meta.sequence: Hero cinematic — The Descent / Sacred Instructions revelation

scenes.name: The call
acceptanceCriteria: Threshold occupies roughly 5% of frame height or less
No light or glow emitting from the threshold
A first-time viewer does not immediately notice the threshold
No runes, carvings, or decorative marks on any rock surface

scenes.name: Recognition
acceptanceCriteria: Only the threshold differs from scene_01 — same cliff, same light, same trail
Glow is visible only inside hairline fractures, not illuminating surroundings
Dust/pebble fall is subtle, not a visual effect shower
No new elements introduced beyond fractures, dust, heat shimmer

scenes.name: Threshold
acceptanceCriteria: Membrane reads as calm liquid light, not lightning or lava-crack texture
Surrounding basalt remains weathered and ordinary, no new decoration
Membrane parts only in final ~15% of shot
Ripple motion is slow sine-wave, not turbulent

scenes.name: Crossing
acceptanceCriteria: Light behaves like thick warm fog/liquid, not plasma or energy
No vortex, spin, or radial particle burst
Obsidian frame visible only at extreme edges
Motion speed matches prior scenes exactly — no acceleration

scenes.name: The descent
scenes.cameraHeight_m: descending — first scene where height changes
acceptanceCriteria: Suggested faces/silhouettes come from natural shadow and erosion only
No carved or sculpted features a reviewer can point to as literal
No floating symbols or decorative particle effects beyond steam
Light sources are natural (shafts from above) only

scenes.name: Revelation
acceptanceCriteria: Only the monolith surface carries golden geometric detail — walls stay unornamented polished stone
Final frame has clean reserved space for future personalization overlay
Camera deceleration is the only scene where pacing intentionally changes
Chamber reads as monumental and quiet, not ornate

changeLog.note: Manifest created. All scenes reset to not_started — the four supplied reference stills did not pass acceptance criteria and are retained as color references only (see master-brief.md intake note).

scene_01 generated fresh (no prior approved anchor found in repo — confirmed with human operator before generating). Higgsfield Nano Banana Pro, 2K, 16:9, 2 credits, job 882d7c20-b4c7-4743-a107-2d2328094d27. Saved to film/stills/scene_01.png as a candidate; status set to still_in_progress, pending Agent 2 review against acceptance criteria.

Agent 2 review of scene_01 candidate (job 882d7c20). VERDICT: regenerate. All four acceptanceCriteria checked individually. FAIL 'Threshold occupies roughly 5% of frame height or less' — measured ring diameter ~165px in a 1536px-tall frame = ~10.7%, over 2x the budget. FAIL 'A first-time viewer does not immediately notice the threshold' — the ring is the only closed regular geometric contour in an otherwise entirely irregular frame; the rim is continuous and near-perfectly circular over its full circumference, which erosion does not produce. It reads as a sealed door/hatch, i.e. prompt-bible's explicit 'obvious gate' fail condition. PASS 'No light or glow emitting from the threshold' — interior is neutral-to-darker than the surrounding sunlit rock; the rim's slight cool cast is shadow/bounce, not emission. PASS 'No runes, carvings, or decorative marks on any rock surface' — no glyphs or inscriptions anywhere in frame. Holistic art-direction.md read: geology, columnar jointing, fumaroles, snow peaks and wet black sand are strong Villeneuve/NatGeo realism with no AI-art tell — retain this base environment on the re-roll. Status left at still_in_progress; scene_02 untouched. Regeneration is Agent 1's task on next handoff.
